notes: BG 2.12 is the central Advaita / Vishishtadvaita battleground of the chapter — Shankara reads the plurals as concession to body-level plurality with non-dual Self underneath, Ramanuja reads them as genuine ontological multiplicity. The engineering layer takes great care to remain at the level both traditions accept: that what is being pointed at (the durable customer requirement) is eternal, regardless of whether one ontology treats it as singular under the surface or as genuinely plural. The analog speaks of 'the underlying need' / 'the durable demand' without committing to whether there is one such substrate per requirement-category, or many distinct ones, or a non-dual ground. The disagreement_among_translators entry explicitly names this as the central commentarial axis and directs the engineering layer not to take a position. The verse's instruction is followed precisely. No tradition-conflation. PASS.
